document.title: AKTI ERP Phase 2 Autonomous Run — Postmortem Report
document.purpose: Preserve the postmortem of the AKTI ERP Phase 2 autonomous Codex run, including patterns, process failures, corrections, autonomy model, and future improvements.
document.scope: Phase 2 autonomous run from control-document setup through completion at P2C-GATE.

executive_summary.summary: The Phase 2 autonomous run was successful, but not frictionless. It began as a control-heavy, manual-review workflow and evolved into a stable autonomous execution model where Codex owns the ticket loop while humans/ChatGPT own phase-level direction, governance decisions, hard-gate review, and final acceptance.
executive_summary.outcome: Codex completed Phase 2 autonomously through P2C-GATE after the runtime-loop, repair-policy, validation-wiring, and governance models were corrected.
executive_summary.main_lesson: Autonomy requires a stable contract and flexible runtime.
executive_summary.before_model: Human validates every step -> Codex executes one ticket -> human updates docs -> Codex continues.
executive_summary.after_model: Codex plans, implements, validates, repairs, commits, pushes, and continues; humans intervene only for hard gates, governance issues, or final audit.

phase_2_scope.phase_2c: WhatsApp Lead Desk Integration through Engagement Gateway Lite
phase_2_scope.phase_2c_limit: Stub/non-production only, no production credentials, no real outbound WhatsApp messages, and no direct Lead Desk-to-Meta/WhatsApp coupling.

timeline.what_happened: Initial validation found Phase 2 control docs were untracked and therefore not repo authority.
timeline.correction: Created, pushed, reviewed, and merged housekeeping/control-doc branches so Phase 2 docs became committed repo authority.
timeline.lesson: Codex must work from repo state, not untracked local files or chat memory.

timeline.what_happened: Codex found mismatches: Phase 1-only registry verification, missing Phase 2 screen-contract validation, broad write globs, branch-base mismatch, and unresolved Phase 2C governance.
timeline.correction: Added P2-VAL-001, P2-VAL-002, exact-file planning, migration policy, branch-base correction, and Phase 2C governance rules.
timeline.lesson: Readiness validation finds cross-file runtime mismatches before implementation.

timeline.what_happened: Control docs still referred to the run as a stress test/experiment.
timeline.correction: Renamed the run as Phase 2 Autonomous Execution Run and changed branch to phase2/autonomous-full-run.
timeline.lesson: Control docs should avoid experimental language that may bias Codex.

timeline.what_happened: Codex refused to proceed under danger-full-access, unrestricted network, or approval-never posture.
timeline.correction: Reconfigured to workspace-write, approval-gated, restricted/no-network style posture with no secret access.
timeline.lesson: Autonomous does not mean unsafe. Environment constraints are part of the run contract.

timeline.stage: Ticket pack and v3 queue creation
timeline.what_happened: Initial execution pack had control-level tickets too broad for implementation, especially Phase 2B.
timeline.correction: Created ticket pack, v2 plan, and v3 queue with split Phase 2B tickets, autonomy statuses, decision rules, and hard gates.
timeline.lesson: Autonomy depends on executable ticket design, not broad control-level task titles.

timeline.what_happened: Codex implemented Engagement Gateway Lite contracts and Phase 2-aware registry verification.
timeline.correction: Reviewed early boundary tickets manually, then moved toward wider autonomy.
timeline.lesson: Early architecture-boundary tickets deserve review, but the system should not require manual review forever.

timeline.what_happened: Control docs mixed stable contract state with live execution ledger fields, causing repeated drift.
timeline.correction: P2-CTRL-003 redefined control docs as stable contract and runtime progress as derived from git history, journal, artifacts, optional run-state.json, and queue order.
timeline.lesson: Do not use control docs as the live execution ledger.

timeline.what_happened: ADR-0003 blocked Phase 2C because pilot date was TBD.
timeline.correction: P2-CTRL-004 recorded a formal stub/non-production exception in ADR-0003 and control docs.
timeline.lesson: Governance decisions must be recorded in repo authority before Codex can act on them.

timeline.what_happened: After the exception, Phase 2C tickets still had stale placeholder fields.
timeline.correction: P2-CTRL-005 replaced stale placeholders with concrete executable stub/non-production scopes and validations.
timeline.lesson: When governance state changes, executable fields must be concretely updated.

timeline.what_happened: Codex stopped on deterministic schema/type/contract validation errors safely fixable inside ticket scope.
timeline.correction: P2-CTRL-006 allowed up to three autonomous repair cycles for local deterministic validation failures inside active ticket scope.
timeline.lesson: Validation should guide repair, not automatically stop autonomy.

timeline.what_happened: Codex stopped because new Phase 2 tests required package test-runner wiring outside the active ticket's original file scope.
timeline.correction: Added policy allowing scoped validation/test-runner wiring updates when required to run active-ticket tests.
timeline.lesson: Tickets that add tests must be allowed to wire those tests into validation.

timeline.what_happened: Codex completed through P2C-GATE with full validation and final audit package.
timeline.correction: No correction required; final verdict was RUN_COMPLETED.
timeline.lesson: Once stable contract, runtime state, repair policy, and validation-wiring policy were in place, full autonomy became practical.

patterns_observed.pattern: Control docs acting as live execution ledger
patterns_observed.symptom: Repo reality advanced after ticket completion, but control docs still pointed to the previous next ticket.
patterns_observed.impact: Codex stopped even though implementation had succeeded.
patterns_observed.fix: Move live state to journal/artifacts/run-state/git history; control docs remain stable contract.

patterns_observed.pattern: Normal validation errors treated as hard stops
patterns_observed.symptom: Codex stopped on import mismatch, manifest shape mismatch, invalid screen key, duplicate action key, and missing capability key.
patterns_observed.impact: Autonomous run required repeated human intervention for repairable errors.
patterns_observed.fix: Add autonomous validation repair policy with a bounded three-cycle repair budget.

patterns_observed.symptom: New tests existed but package test script did not run them, and package.json was outside ticket scope.
patterns_observed.impact: Codex stopped before implementing valid ticket tests.
patterns_observed.fix: Allow scoped validation/test-runner wiring updates inside relevant package files.

patterns_observed.symptom: Early process generated many zips, plans, and handoff artifacts.
patterns_observed.fix: Use lightweight per-ticket summaries and changed-files zips; reserve heavy audit for phase/final gates.

patterns_observed.symptom: P2A-GATE and P2B-GATE were initially treated as human review stops even if passed.
patterns_observed.fix: P2B-GATE became pass-through on validation pass; P2C-GATE became final completion gate.

patterns_observed.symptom: User authorized Phase 2C, but ADR-0003 remained blocking.
patterns_observed.impact: Codex could not proceed into Phase 2C.
patterns_observed.fix: Record formal exception in ADR-0003 and update control docs.

codex_autonomous_loop: repair local deterministic failures within repair budget
human_chatgpt_role: keep architecture and business direction aligned

control_doc_corrections.purpose: Adopt v3 autonomous queue in active control docs.
Mark P2-VAL-001 complete and move start marker to P2A-002.
Simplify autonomous runtime loop and separate stable contract from runtime ledger.
Record ADR-0003 Phase 2C stub/non-production exception and unlock Phase 2C under constraints.
Clean stale Phase 2C governance placeholder fields.
Allow scoped test-runner wiring updates when needed to run active-ticket tests.

phase_2a: Engagement Gateway Lite contracts and manifest boundary
Engagement Gateway persistence decision via deferral path
phase_2b: Lead Desk contract and capability boundary
Lead Desk data model decision and schema/registry foundation
phase_2c: Gateway-mediated WhatsApp stub provider runtime integration

what_went_well: Codex respected boundaries and stopped on real policy issues.
The v3 queue turned broad tickets into executable autonomous work.
Repair policy unlocked practical autonomy without weakening safety.
Validation/test-runner wiring policy allowed tests to evolve with Phase 2.
Phase 2C was handled safely as stub/non-production with governance recorded.
what_did_not_go_well: Too many control-doc corrections were required before stable autonomy emerged.
Plan Mode was overused after v3 became stable.
Evidence burden was too heavy early in the run.
Gate semantics were unclear until late in the process.
Test wiring policy was not included at the start.
Phase 2C governance exception had to be added late.

process_improvements.improvement: Separate contract, queue, and runtime state from day one.
process_improvements.details: Use run_contract, ticket_queue, and run_state as separate concepts/files.

process_improvements.improvement: Include autonomous validation repair policy from the start.
process_improvements.details: Allow bounded repair of deterministic local validation failures inside ticket scope.

process_improvements.improvement: Include validation/test-runner wiring policy from the start.
process_improvements.details: Allow active ticket to wire its own tests into package validation without dependency additions or unrelated script rewrites.
Each gate should be marked as pass-through, human-review-stop, or final-stop.
Use summary.md and changed-files.zip per ticket; use heavy audit only at phase/final gates.
Check stale placeholders, governance blocks, scripts, test wiring, queue scope, and missing validation tooling.
Complete means validated, tested, contract-backed, no fake data, no placeholder UX, and no skipped enforcement, while still not inventing unapproved business rules.

recommended_future_phase_model.control_doc_strategy: Create one mature control-doc commit before /goal instead of many iterative correction commits.
recommended_future_phase_model.dry_run_strategy: Run one final autonomy-blocker dry run before /goal.
recommended_future_phase_model.goal_strategy: Run /goal once with stable contract and allow Codex to continue until hard gate or final gate.

key_lessons: Autonomy needs a stable contract and flexible runtime.
Strict boundaries should not prevent local deterministic repairs.
Control docs must not become live state ledgers.
Governance must be committed to repo authority, not left in chat.
Evidence supports delivery; it must not become the deliverable.
Codex can operate autonomously when the queue, repair model, runtime state, and stop policy are designed correctly.

post_merge_status.authority_note: This postmortem is historical/process learning. It is not an active Phase 3 or Phase 4 execution contract.

next_steps: Create external audit package for the completed Phase 2 branch.
Run external audit against contracts, Prisma, registry, API, frontend, and Phase 2C stub boundaries.
Decide whether branch is merge-candidate, needs hardening, or should be split into PRs.
Convert final autonomous operating model into reusable docs/process/AUTONOMOUS_RUNBOOK.md.
